fields.Text:
2-year-old with facial weakness, somnolence, nausea/vomiting. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Brainstem glioma}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Diffuse intrinsic pontine/brainstem glioma appears as an expansile infiltrative tumor centered in the pons, extending to middle cerebellar peduncles, midbrain, and medulla. It shows hyperintensity on T2-weighted and FLAIR images with minimal or no enhancement. The tumor frequently engulfs the basilar artery and shows exophytic growth into the prepontine cistern.




Open review Q1

fields.Text: Diffuse intrinsic pontine/brainstem glioma appears as an expansile infiltrative pontine mass with {{c1::T2/FLAIR hyperintensity and minimal or no enhancement}}.
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Diffuse intrinsic pontine/brainstem glioma appears as an expansile infiltrative tumor centered in the pons, extending to middle cerebellar peduncles, midbrain, and medulla. It shows hyperintensity on T2-weighted and FLAIR images with minimal or no enhancement. The tumor frequently engulfs the basilar artery and shows exophytic growth into the prepontine cistern.








Open review Q1

fields.Text:
Older adult with elbow pain and destructive bone lesion. Most likely cause?

{{c1::Metastatic disease}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Frontal radiograph of the left humerus shows a mottled ill-defined lytic lesion of the distal humeral metaphysis (yellow arrow).




Open review Q2

fields.Text: In an older adult, an aggressive painful bone lesion is most often {{c1::metastatic disease}} until proven otherwise.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Frontal radiograph of the left humerus shows a mottled ill-defined lytic lesion of the distal humeral metaphysis (yellow arrow).

fields.Text:
Bone scan with elevated alkaline phosphatase as only lab abnormality. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Paget disease}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q3. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tc-99m-MDP is highly sensitive but not specific for Paget's disease. It is useful to define the overall extent and distribution of disease. There is marked increased uptake in all phases of the disease, although in the burnt-out sclerotic quiescent phase uptake may be normal. There may also be the “Mickey Mouse sign,” showing uptake in the vertebral body and posterior elements forming an inverted triangular pattern on posterior planar imaging resembling a Mickey Mouse silhouette. It is also known as the heart or clover sign and T or champagne glass sign. The “Lincoln sign” refers to diffuse mandibular uptake forming a bearded appearance.




Open review Q3

fields.Text: Paget disease on bone scan shows {{c1::marked increased uptake in involved bone}} and may present with isolated alkaline phosphatase elevation.
fields.Extra:
Q3. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tc-99m-MDP is highly sensitive but not specific for Paget's disease. It is useful to define the overall extent and distribution of disease. There is marked increased uptake in all phases of the disease, although in the burnt-out sclerotic quiescent phase uptake may be normal. There may also be the “Mickey Mouse sign,” showing uptake in the vertebral body and posterior elements forming an inverted triangular pattern on posterior planar imaging resembling a Mickey Mouse silhouette. It is also known as the heart or clover sign and T or champagne glass sign. The “Lincoln sign” refers to diffuse mandibular uptake forming a bearded appearance.

fields.Text: Bone scan tracer uptake is by {{c1::chemical adsorption}}, not sodium-iodide symporter uptake.

fields.Text: For CTDIvol, doubling pitch while keeping other CT parameters constant {{c1::halves dose}}.
fields.Extra:
Q10. Correct; difficulty: not listed. CTDIvol = CTDIw / Pitch Pitch is calculated by dividing table distance traveled in 1 gantry rotation by slice thickness.

fields.Text:
Knee radiograph/MRI with superolateral patellar fragment and no marrow edema. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Bipartite patella}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: easy. A bipartite patella is a normal developmental variation in which the superior lateral ossification center of the patella fails to fuse.




Open review Q11

fields.Text: Bipartite patella is favored over fracture when the fragment is well-corticated and {{c1::lacks marrow edema}}.
fields.Extra:

fields.Text: Gamma camera intrinsic uniformity tests the detector without collimator; extrinsic uniformity tests the complete system and is performed {{c1::daily}}.
fields.Extra:
Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Intrinsic uniformity tests detector alone using distant point source without collimator. Extrinsic uniformity tests complete system with collimator using flood sources - performed daily (extrinsic) and weekly (intrinsic) with 5 to 15 million counts.




Open review Q12

fields.Text:
Recurrent UTI with cystic structure at posterior bladder/trigone. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Ureterocele}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Ureteroceles appear as thin-walled cystic structures arising from the posterior bladder wall at the trigone, with no internal vascularity on color Doppler. Ureterocele is associated with duplication of the renal collecting system.

fields.Text: A ureterocele appears as a {{c1::thin-walled cystic structure at the posterior bladder wall/trigone}} and is associated with duplicated collecting systems.

{{c1::Angiomyolipoma}}

fields.Extra:
Q14. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: India ink artifact at the renal mass-kidney interface and loss of lesion signal on out of phase imaging both indicate fat within the lesion, which is diagnostic of angiomyolipoma.




Open review Q14

fields.Text: Macroscopic fat in a renal mass, including India-ink artifact or out-of-phase signal loss, is diagnostic of {{c1::angiomyolipoma}}.

fields.Text:

{{c1::Nasopharyngeal carcinoma}}
fields.Extra:
Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Nasopharyngeal carcinoma is the most common type of primary nasopharyngeal malignancy, occurring most frequently in Asia. Nodal metastases are usually present at the time of diagnosis, usually found effacing the fossa of Rosenmuller. Level II, V, and retropharyngeal nodes are commonly involved.

{{c1::Chiari II malformation}}

1/6
fields.Extra:
Q19. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Chiari II malformation shows reliable sagittal plane features including downward cerebellar herniation, downward displacement of medulla and fourth ventricle, medullary kinking, beaked tectum, and hypoplastic tentorium. Small fourth ventricle is characteristic.

fields.Text: Chiari II malformation is associated with a small posterior fossa and {{c1::downward displacement of the cerebellum/medulla/fourth ventricle}}.

fields.Text:
Large sacral CSF-signal perineural cystic lesion. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Tarlov cyst}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q20. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Tarlov cysts are cerebrospinal fluid-filled perineural cysts with nerve root fibers in their walls that commonly erode and remodel sacral bone. Large cysts extending beyond the posterior sacrum can cause radicular pain, neurological deficits, and bowel or bladder dysfunction requiring percutaneous fibrin sealant injection or surgical intervention.

fields.Text: Tarlov cysts are {{c1::CSF-filled perineural cysts}} that can remodel sacral bone and cause radicular symptoms.

{{c1::Staphylococcus aureus}}

fields.Text: In developed countries, the most common cause of pediatric vertebral osteomyelitis/discitis is {{c1::Staphylococcus aureus}}.

fields.Text: Acute allergic-like reactions to gadolinium contrast are treated {{c1::the same way as iodinated contrast reactions}}.
fields.Extra:
Q22. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The key concept is that treatment of gadolinium-based contrast reactions is identical to that of iodinated contrast reactions, with immediate hypersensitivity reactions managed using standard anaphylaxis protocols, including intramuscular epinephrine as first-line therapy, airway support, and adjunctive medications as indicated.

{{c1::Not diagnostic of adenoma; evaluate for metastasis}}

fields.Text: An adrenal nodule that does not lose signal on out-of-phase MRI is {{c1::not diagnostic of lipid-rich adenoma}} and may need metastasis workup.

fields.Text:
Hyperthyroidism with heterogeneous thyroid uptake. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Toxic multinodular goiter}}
fields.Extra:
Q24. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Multinodular goiter scintigraphy with 123I or 99mTc-pertechnetate demonstrates heterogeneous tracer distribution. Hot nodules show increased uptake and may suppress surrounding thyroid tissue. Cold nodules appear as photopenic defects with decreased or absent tracer uptake. The overall pattern contrasts with normal thyroid's homogeneous tracer distribution.

fields.Text: Toxic multinodular goiter shows {{c1::heterogeneous thyroid tracer uptake}} rather than diffuse homogeneous uptake.

The overall pattern contrasts with normal thyroid's homogeneous tracer distribution.

{{c1::Splenic flexure}}

1/5
fields.Extra:
Q33. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: A positive nuclear medicine GI bleeding scan shows a focal site of increased activity within the abdomen or pelvis, which progresses distally in the bowel. Since blood is an irritant to the intestines, movement of activity within the bowel loops is often rapid and may be bidirectional.

Adolescent with acute pelvic pain and absent ovarian Doppler flow. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Ovarian torsion}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q34. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Ovarian torsion shows enlarged ovary with peripherally dispersed follicles and heterogeneous echotexture. Key findings include lack of vascularity on color Doppler and the whirlpool sign, which appears as a hypoechoic band representing the twisted pedicle with vessels looping around the center axis.

fields.Text: The most common cause of benign bile duct stricture is {{c1::iatrogenic injury during laparoscopic cholecystectomy}}.

fields.Text: A solid nodular component in biliary mucinous cystic neoplasm suggests {{c1::malignant transformation/invasive carcinoma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q37. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The old terms biliary cystadenoma versus cystadenocarcinoma are now understood as a spectrum within mucinous cystic neoplasm from low-grade dysplasia to high-grade dysplasia to mucinous cystic neoplasm with invasive carcinoma, with solid nodular components suggesting malignant transformation.

fields.Text: FDG is phosphorylated by {{c1::hexokinase}}, not tyrosine kinase, and is trapped as FDG-6-phosphate.
fields.Extra:
Q38. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Hexokinase phosphorylates fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG) after cellular uptake, trapping it as FDG-6-phosphate. The degree of FDG retention in cells directly reflects hexokinase activity and thus local glycolytic rate and is the basis for FDG PET imaging.

fields.Text:
CT with postprandial abdominal pain and venous mesenteric abnormality. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Superior mesenteric vein thrombosis}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Superior mesenteric vein thrombosis presents with nonspecific symptoms requiring high clinical suspicion. CT angiography is diagnostic, and immediate anticoagulation dramatically reduces mortality compared to delayed treatment.

fields.Text:

{{c1::Chondrocalcinosis}}
fields.Extra:
Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Chondrocalcinosis on knee radiographs appears as punctate and linear calcifications within the meniscal fibrocartilage and articular cartilage, representing calcium pyrophosphate deposition disease, with lateral compartment involvement more common than medial.

fields.Text:
3-month-old with seizures and smooth thick cortex. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Lissencephaly}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q44. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Lissencephaly demonstrates a smooth brain surface with thick cortex and thin subcortical white matter. A characteristic circumferential high T2 signal band appears in the parieto-occipital cortex representing a sparse cell zone. Complete lissencephaly shows agyria while incomplete forms demonstrate parieto-occipital agyria with frontotemporal pachygyria.




Open review Q44

fields.Text: Lissencephaly shows a smooth brain surface with {{c1::thick cortex and thin subcortical white matter}}.

A characteristic circumferential high T2 signal band appears in the parieto-occipital cortex representing a sparse cell zone. Complete lissencephaly shows agyria while incomplete forms demonstrate parieto-occipital agyria with frontotemporal pachygyria.

fields.Text:
Infant skeletal survey concerning for nonaccidental trauma. Radiologist obligation?

{{c1::Report to Child Protective Services}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The radiologist's role includes alerting clinicians to specificity of imaging findings and providing guidance when findings are concerning for abuse. Multiple healing fractures in a young infant with an implausible history represent a classic presentation requiring immediate CPS involvement to ensure child safety and proper investigation.

fields.Text: When imaging is concerning for child abuse, the radiologist must {{c1::report the case to Child Protective Services}} even if another clinician disagrees.

{{c1::Fahr disease}}

fields.Extra:

These calcifications occur alongside basal ganglia involvement and manifest with cerebellar dysfunction including ataxia and speech difficulties.

fields.Text:
Gastric band with abnormal phi angle. Position problem?

{{c1::Band malposition}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q49. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The drawing below shows how the phi angle is measured to determine whether a gastric band is correctly positioned. The phi angle is the angle formed by the profile of the gastric band and the vertical axis of the spine on frontal views. If the band is correctly positioned, the phi angle will be between 4° and 58°. Note that the connector tube is not visible because an older model of the REALIZE system was used in this patient.

fields.Text: Normal gastric band phi angle is approximately {{c1::4-58 degrees}} relative to the vertical axis of the spine.

fields.Text:
Recent fall on anticoagulation with thigh swelling and fluid-fluid levels. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Intramuscular hemorrhage}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q53. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Fluid-fluid levels (hematocrit effect) show linear separation of cellular and liquid blood components, representing a highly sensitive sign of coagulopathic hemorrhage rather than exclusively traumatic etiology. This pattern strongly suggests a component of anticoagulation-related bleeding.

fields.Text: Initial imaging for a palpable breast mass in a woman under 30 is {{c1::targeted ultrasound}}.
fields.Extra:
Q54. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Breast ultrasound (U/S)is the preferred method of evaluation of a palpable breast mass in a woman under 30. If breast U/S identifies a solid lesion with sonographic features suggestive of a fibroadenoma (ie, oval circumscribed hypoechoic mass), this may be assessed as BI-RADS 3 lesion that is probably benign. Repeat U/S imaging in 6 months is a reasonable option in lieu of biopsy for a BI-RADS-3 lesion.




Open review Q54

fields.Text: Uncomplicated acute rhinosinusitis usually requires {{c1::no initial imaging}} because imaging does not distinguish viral from bacterial disease.
fields.Extra:
Q55. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Imaging generally doesn't change initial treatment for uncomplicated acute rhinosinusitis because it can't differentiate between bacterial and viral etiologies.

fields.Text: An indicative biomarker for probable dementia with Lewy bodies is {{c1::reduced dopamine transporter uptake in the basal ganglia on SPECT/PET}}.
fields.Extra:
Q57. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Current diagnostic criteria for probably dementia with Lewy bodies (DLB) include cognitive impairment and two or more core clinical features, which include fluctuating cognition, REM sleep behavior disorder, visual hallucinations, and parkinsonism. If only a single core feature is present, a positive indicative biomarker is enough to diagnose probable DLB. These biomarkers include reduced dopamine transporter uptake in the basal ganglia on SPECT or PET, low 123iodine-MIBG uptake on myocardial scintigraphy, and a polysomnogram demonstrating REM sleep without atonia.




Open review Q57

fields.Text: Pure LCIS without a mass lesion is managed by considering excision {{c1::in the clinical/radiologic context}} rather than automatic mandatory excision in all cases.
fields.Extra:

{{c1::Microatheroma/hyalinization and arteriolar sclerosis}}

Q59. Correct; difficulty: hard. Lacunar infarctions represent small, deep infarcts that arise from occlusion of small penetrating end arteries (lenticulostriate, thalamo-perforating, pontine perforating arteries, and recurrent artery of Heubner).
